tabSummaries.summary: Profile tab persists through two distinct paths: _saveSettings (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:2941) writes ONLY name/calcom/digest/dormant to Firebase Auth + Firestore users/{uid}, with load handled by _loadProfileSettings fired from goTo('settings') -> switchSettingsTab('profile') (dashboard-actions.js:460, ui.js:992-996); the four other profile inputs (Company/Phone/Role/License) are confirmed-dead placeholders per the in-code comment at dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:2931-2937. Pipeline preferences save instantly to localStorage via data-on-change wrappers (all allowlisted), but the bold/auto-collapse checkbox and density active-state loaders live in a bare DOMContentLoaded+200ms listener (dashboard-ui.js:1589-1601) that fires before tpl-view-settings hydrates — the PR #597 template-rehydration pattern — so those controls never display their saved state, while crmSecHeaderToggle alone gets a proper goTo-hook sync (dashboard-actions.js:1090-1093). Gotchas: the Joe AI key is sessionStorage nbd_joe_key (not localStorage as the ledger says), the cal.com live preview updates text but never href (dashboard-ui-prefs-boot.js:272-275), and Load Sample Data writes 13 prod leads with zero confirm gate.

The Estimates tab is a single-writer design: individual inputs have NO per-field listeners — everything persists only when the Save All button fires _saveEstimateDefaultsV2 (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:3077), which writes the whole patch to localStorage nbd_est_settings_v3 via EstimateBuilderV2.updateSettings, mirrors it to Firestore userSettings/{uid}.estimateSettingsV2 (WRITE-ONLY — nothing in the repo ever reads that field back, so the claimed cross-device sync is one-way dead; worth a finding), and separately writes the v2addon* fields shop-wide to companyProfile.pricing.addonPrices. Load happens on tab-switch (ui.js:944-953) after the lazy 'estimates' bundle, reading the same LS key, so local round-trip is sound for every field in the patch map. Three controls — #defPitch, #defWaste, #defDeckPct — are markup-only ghosts (zero JS references repo-wide): Save All silently discards them and reload reverts them to hardcoded HTML defaults; the engine derives pitch/waste per-estimate instead. Calc-time gotcha for asserting take-effect: applyCompanyPricing (estimate-builder-v2.js:485-506) lets companyProfile.pricing override tierRates/dumpFee/tearOffExtraPerSq/addonPrices over the LS values, so form round-trip can pass while a shop-profile value governs actual pricing.

The Daily OS tab is entirely client-side: dsSaveConfig (dashboard-actions.js:1025-1058) writes three localStorage keys in one click — nbd_user_config (canonical config), nbd_ds_config (Home-widget mirror added by the NEW-4 fix at :1039-1053), and ds-theme (program theme) — so the driver must snapshot all three before testing and restore byte-for-byte after. Load is dsLoadConfig fired from a goTo('settings') wrapper with an 80ms setTimeout (dashboard-actions.js:1086-1088), so reload-verification must re-enter settings via the SPA router (#/settings) rather than expecting fields populated at raw page load; the floor editor and theme grid are JS-built but genuinely wired (direct addEventListener at dashboard-ui.js:2076-2094 and the data-du-action delegate at :2429-2444; programmatic value-sets need an 'input' dispatch or dsFloors stays stale). One real suspect: the Program Theme swatch round-trips to ds-theme correctly, but its only runtime consumers (daily-success app.js:1390, maps.js:487) read ds-theme as last fallback behind nbd_pro_theme/nbd-theme — which the dashboard theme engine always writes — and the daily-success lookup table lacks 8 of the 12 swatch keys, so the picked program theme almost never takes effect.

The Company tab is a clean, uniform 18-field block: every input is in CO_FIELDS (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:3200-3204); _saveCompanySettings (:3206) writes the whole object to localStorage nbd_company_settings (:3212) AND Firestore userSettings/{uid}.company merge (:3216-3220); _loadCompanySettings (:3233) reads the same LS key then lets Firestore win, and runs automatically when the tab opens via switchSettingsTab('company') (ui.js:956-958) — so after a reload the driver MUST re-click #stab-company (dashboard.html:9779) before asserting field values, and should allow ~2s for the async Firestore read to settle. Both functions are on _NBD_CALL_ALLOWLIST (dashboard-state.js:201/:215) and dispatched by the bound data-action="call" delegate (dashboard-ui.js:490-500), and they live in a module, not the rehydrating template, so none of the CSP/DCL dead-control patterns apply: round-trip is sound for all 19 rows. The important systemic finding is TAKE-EFFECT: nothing in the codebase consumes nbd_company_settings or userSettings.company at runtime — doc-gen letterhead/license/phone come from the separate companyProfile/main (Company Profile tab cp_* fields) and document-generator.js has zero references to any co* field — so the entire tab is store-only data; it saves and reloads correctly but changing it alters no document, map, or widget output.

Company Profile tab is a single-form, single-save design: every cp_* input is read in one pass by _cpReadFormToProfile (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:3276) when the Save button calls _saveCompanyProfileSettings (:3365), which writes the WHOLE form via window._saveCompanyProfile (company-profile.js:276) to Firestore doc companyProfile/{companyId-claim||uid} (key from _resolveCompanyKey :222 — NOT literally 'main' since the tenancy fix) with setDoc merge:true (:298), plus an LS mirror nbd_company_profile_v1 (:287) that gives an instant, reload-free assertion target. Load is symmetric and fires on every tab open: switchSettingsTab('company-profile') at ui.js:962-965 calls _loadCompanyProfileSettings (bootstrap:3355) → fresh getDoc (company-profile.js:263) → _cpPopulateFormFromProfile (bootstrap:3324), so the driver must WAIT for async populate before recording originals. Gotchas: (1) this is a LIVE shop-wide doc — every saved token prints on real customer contracts until restored, so each test must record-original→token→save→verify→restore-original→save; (2) Save persists ALL fields, so a first-ever save materializes default values into Firestore (benign — values identical to fallbacks); (3) services/valueProps arrays skip all-blank rows on save (bootstrap:3311/3319) so slot indexes can shift — assert by content not index; (4) financingTiers/services/valueProps arrays are replaced wholesale (deepMerge company-profile.js:182/189 returns source.slice(), matching Firestore array-replace semantics) — no array-merge mismatch. Two fields (cancellationStatute, proposalValidityDays) round-trip perfectly but have NO runtime consumer in the entire repo.

The Notifications tab is a single-save-button form: _saveNotifSettings (dashboard-bootstrap.module.js:3495-3529) reads the notifMode radio group + 7 trigger + 4 channel checkboxes by element id and writes one JSON blob to LS nbd_notif_settings, mirrored to Firestore userSettings/{uid}.notifications; load is symmetric via switchSettingsTab('notifications') (ui.js:980-984) -> _loadNotifSettings -> _applyNotifSettingsToUI reading the same key (plus a one-shot async Firestore re-sync at :3564-3579 — drivers should wait ~1s after opening the tab before asserting checkbox state). No CSP dead controls: both buttons use the document-level data-action="call" delegate (dashboard-ui.js:200) and both fns are on _NBD_CALL_ALLOWLIST (dashboard-state.js:200-201), and individual checkboxes intentionally have no own listeners (save-button pattern). The big finding: only TWO call sites consume the saved settings (tasks.js createNotification for task_overdue, review-engine.js:126 for estimate_approved), so five controls persist but gate nothing — notifHot (ask-joe-proactive builds hot_lead briefings without the gate), notifStorm (storm-integration.js:356-394 toasts/queues directly), notifInbound and notifNeedsField (no firing code exists at all), and chEmail (no client or Cloud Function reads the email channel flag). All five are flagged mismatch-suspect; their LS round-trips still pass.
